title: lichen_l2_recv returns NET_CONTINUE unconditionally with no processing
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 118-125: The L2 recv callback just returns NET_CONTINUE because decompression happens in lichen_l2_input() before net_recv_data() is called. The comment explains this ('Packet is already an IPv6 packet'). Consider whether this callback could be NULL/removed, or whether Zephyr's L2 contract requires it. If it must exist, the comment is sufficient.

Rewrite if removable: Set recv callback to NULL in NET_L2_INIT if the API allows.
close_reason: Correct L2 pattern: decompression happens before net_recv_data() call; recv callback returning NET_CONTINUE with explanatory comment is the standard pattern when L2 doesn't modify the packet
